text:
rahara mean the same.
In ancient past the word Ya ma meant 60/8 = 7.5 Khati (24/
8 = 3 hours). Thus in ancient past the words Ya ma and Prahara
meant 3 hours of time. Half of it was known as Yamardha
meaning 'half ofYama' which was equivalent to 60/16 = 3.75
Khati =1.5 hours.
But with time this definition changed. Yamardha started
to get known by the name 'Yama'. i.e. By the period of
Prasnanushtana Padhati, Prasnamarga, Madhaveeya etc the
word Y ama meant 1.5 hours and the meaning of the word
Prahara remained as 3 hours itself. That is why Prasnamarga
defines Y ama as l/8th of a day, which is equal to 1 hour 30
minutes.
1 Ahoratra (1 Vara) = 60 Khati = 24 hours
1 Day = 1 Night= 30 Khati = 12 hours
This definition is valid only for equinox day (Vishu). On
all other days necessary correction should be applied based
on length of day and night. Thus span of Ya ma is also not
constant. As the length of day and night varies the length of
Y ama will also vary accordingly.
